items.title: Preferred bidder announced for troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter

children.content: A consortium headed by Adroit Capital is announced as the preferred bidder for the smelter in northern Tasmania, as federal and state government pledge another $5 million to support workers in the weeks ahead.

gallery.altText: A large smelter looking dark and deserted.

thumbnail.altText: A large smelter looking dark and deserted.

items.title: Hungry eleven-armed seastars making a meal of Tasmanian scallops

children.content: The fast spread of pest eleven-armed seastars has fishers, along with researchers, concerned.

image.altText: A seastar at Hallett Cove.

items.title: Tasmania 'just wanted to roll trucks in' as quick fix for icebreaker refuelling saga

children.content: A federal department says the Tasmanian government's proposed interim solution to a costly refuelling saga affecting Australia's Antarctic icebreaker is "far from" acceptable.

image.altText: The red-hulled Nuyina next to the dock in Hobart.

items.title: Tasmania's Bonorong Wildlife Sanctuary to star in new docuseries

children.content: It's not all cute and fluffy animal stories at Bonorong. The wildlife sanctuary's director Greg Irons says a new docuseries will show the real face of animal conservation, and it's not all "happy Larry".

gallery.altText: A man holding a wombat and being filmed by a camera man
Man smiles for photo in front of shed that says 'BONORONG'
Two women looking at a computer screen and chatting

items.title: Spirit ferries operator set for major restructure under Tasmanian Labor plan

children.content: The two government businesses responsible for the botched Spirit of Tasmania ship replacement program would undergo a major restructure, with one stripped of all assets, under a Tasmanian Labor government.

image.altText: A large white and red ship docked in Hobart.

items.title: Youth escapee avoids recording conviction for knifepoint carjacking of tourists

children.content: One of the teenagers involved in a group escape from the Ashley Youth Detention Centre in December is sentenced for his involvement in the armed robbery of two tourists.

image.altText: A coastal town along a beach with yellow sand under a partly cloudy sky seen from above.

items.title: 'Intolerable': When $786m bridge was being designed, engineers raised a red flag

children.content: Despite government assurances Hobart's Bridgewater Bridge is safe, there are fresh concerns over its foundation design, with one expert who worked on the project suggesting it could eventually lead to a need to reduce traffic flow or speed limits.

gallery.altText: A silver metal device attached to a concrete pylon of a bridge.
Close up of reinforcing measures in a bridge structure.
An close up of a bridge design drawing, with an engineer pointing to the piles.

thumbnail.altText: A silver metal device attached to a concrete pylon of a bridge.
Close up of reinforcing measures in a bridge structure.
An close up of a bridge design drawing, with an engineer pointing to the piles.

items.title: How does Hobart revitalise its night life?

children.content: The Hobart City Council is looking for fresh ideas to help revitalise the city's nightlife.

items.title: US military strikes Iranian boats and missile launch sites

children.content: US Centcom says the strikes were designed "to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces".

articles.title: Why Trump's push to expand his Abraham Accords was met with silence

children.content: As US President Donald Trump pushes for a deal with Iran, he is reviving one of the signature foreign policy projects of his first term: the Abraham Accords.

image.altText: A photo of Benjamin Netanyahu, Donald Trump and Abdullah bin Zayed sitting at a long table, holding up copies of signed deals

articles.title: Trump calls on Arab nations to 'immediately sign the Abraham Accords'

children.content: Donald Trump has boldly tied negotiations to end his war with Iran to a swathe of Arab nations formally establishing diplomatic ties with Israel.

image.altText: Israeli PM Netanyahu shakes hands with US President Donald Trump, who points at him.

gallery.altText: two us military fighter jets take off over the ocean

thumbnail.altText: two us military fighter jets take off over the ocean

items.title: NACC boss Paul Brereton admits he contributed to own 'downfall'

children.content: The corruption watchdog boss, appearing during a tense grilling at Senate estimates, admits his conduct became a "distraction".

image.altText: A grey-haired man with glasses, looking serious.

items.title: Hypersonic missile attack a sign of Putin's 'bruised ego', expert says

children.content: Russia escalates attacks on Kyiv by using a hypersonic missile in one of its largest assaults on the Ukrainian capital since the start of the four-year war, and warns foreigners to leave the city ahead of further strikes.

image.altText: A man with a bandaged head with blood on his t-shirt pats his dog while sheltering in an underground bomb shelter.

items.title: Archibald Prize sitter calls out APY art collective mistreatment

children.content: Award-winning artist Iluwanti Ken says the withdrawal of funding and support over the "white hands on black art" scandal has left her feeling like a stranger in her own industry.

gallery.altText: A seated Aboriginal woman holds a microphone and gestures with her free hand.
Iluwanti Ken, in her late 70s, stands in front of an intricately detailed black-and-white painting of eagles hung in a gallery.

thumbnail.altText: A seated Aboriginal woman holds a microphone and gestures with her free hand.
Iluwanti Ken, in her late 70s, stands in front of an intricately detailed black-and-white painting of eagles hung in a gallery.

avatar.alt: Hannah Story, a blonde woman sitting outside smiles. A dog is at her feet, and she wears a T-shirt that reads "MIRANDA JULY".

items.title: Kumanjayi White family 'heartbroken' no charges laid over custody death

children.content: The family of Kumanjayi White tell the ABC they are "heartbroken" that no charges will be laid over his death in custody, as Northern Territory Police confirm their criminal investigation has concluded.

image.altText: A group of Aboriginal mourners walking through a coles supermarket, while holding branches.

items.title: One woman will make Japanese history by going on maternity leave

children.content: A Japanese mayor will take maternity leave this year, becoming the first in the country's history to do so while in office.

image.altText: A woman in a grey suit smiles at the camera and holds her fist up in a soccer stadium

items.title: K-pop triumphs at American Music Awards as Pussycat Dolls lead noughties nostalgia

children.content: Taylor Swift led the night with eight nominations but didn't take home a single award, while 80s rock star Billy Idol was honoured with a Lifetime Achievement Award.

gallery.altText: Three women in red latex pose on stage.
Billy Idol in black leather and a purple shirt poses.

Billy Idol in black leather and a purple shirt poses.

items.title: Police taskforce investigating Dezi Freeman arrests two men

children.content: Detectives investigating the movements of police killer Dezi Freeman have arrested two men in Victoria's north-east.

image.altText: Screengrab from footage of Dezi Freeman wearing sunglasses and gesturing while speaking with police.

items.title: Romanian man jailed for stealing from elderly Australian shoppers

children.content: The Romanian citizen, who illegally visited Australia as part of a global "distraction fraud" operation, has been jailed after fleecing thousands of dollars from elderly Australian shoppers.

image.altText: Vasile Bombonel with his face blurred, holding two thumbs up while being escorted by police.

items.title: When moving forward means being the cycle-breaker in a family

children.content: Motherhood has a way of making family patterns impossible to ignore. Suddenly you start asking questions you never asked before.

gallery.altText: Carlie Schofield pictures as a baby, being held by her mum.

thumbnail.altText: Carlie Schofield pictures as a baby, being held by her mum.

avatar.alt: Carlie Schofield wears and maroon top smiling in a room with an Aboriginal artwork on the wall.

items.title: Using AI to manage your budget? This is what experts want you to know

children.content: "Hallucinations", privacy and other factors to consider if you're turning to artificial intelligence for financial advice.

image.altText: The hands hands of a woman typing, while she's reclined on the sofa.

items.title: The many phases of Miles Davis, who would have turned 100 today

children.content: From Illinois to New York to Australia, legendary trumpeter Miles Davis helped shape the direction of jazz with albums like Birth of the Cool and Kind of Blue.

image.altText: A black and white photo of Miles Davis playing the trumpet. His eyes look down and he has a serious expression.

items.title: 'Catastrophic failure' after drones malfunction at Vivid Sydney show

children.content: The incident occurred not long into Monday's 7.30pm "star-bound" show when almost 90 drones began falling out of formation and into the harbour around Cockle Bay.

image.altText: Drones carrying colourful lights form an unidentifiable shape over a bay at night

gallery.altText: Drone lights falling into the water below, a skyscraper in the background.

thumbnail.altText: Drone lights falling into the water below, a skyscraper in the background.

items.title: After losing her 3yo daughter, one mum says MDMA therapy saved her life

children.content: Jess Yugovich is among the Australians trying MDMA-assisted psychotherapy after being diagnosed with PTSD, as highly regulated facilities process the drug for therapeutic use.

image.altText: Woman with short brown hair looks at camera in a green silk shirt and in a well lit therapy room.

items.title: Teary council officer tells ICAC they 'should be ashamed of yourselves'

children.content: Roxanne Thornton accuses Independent Commission Against Corruption of "laying the boot in" after four days of questioning over the manipulation of the recruitment process at the council to secure jobs for friends and associates.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair in maroon and navy top holding a tissue

items.title: Police officer accused of rape denied he was 'sleazy', court told

children.content: A police officer goes on trial in South Australia's District Court after pleading not guilty to two counts of rape and one count of attempted rape.

image.altText: An old stone building with a red dome

items.title: Flights resume at training school after two killed in plane crash

children.content: An aviation school resumes flight operations at Adelaide's Parafield Airport four weeks after an instructor and a student onboard a twin-engine plane were killed when it crashed into a hangar.

image.altText: Black smoke billows from a plane crash at Parafield Airport

items.title: Late pastoralist's family loses appeal over judgement that father was responsible for bushfire

children.content: A court judgement that found pastoralist Tom Brinkworth liable for a 2021 bushfire, five months after he died, has been upheld after an appeal by Mr Brinkworth's family.

gallery.altText: Smoke about the Lucindale grass fire

thumbnail.altText: Smoke about the Lucindale grass fire

items.title: Santos boss 'can't believe' gas reservation would force loss-making prices

children.content: The federal government's draft framework would require gas producers to supply 20 per cent of their exports to the domestic market, with limited exceptions, which gas producers warn would "undermine" future supply.

image.altText: Kevin Gallagher wears a suit at a press conference in Darwin.

items.title: Power price drops for most customers as batteries flatten peak

children.content: Surging levels of renewable energy and better reliability from coal-fired generators are set to give consumers a break, with benchmark power prices to fall up to 10 per cent for consumers and more for small businesses.

image.altText: rows of battery containers at sunset

items.title: Israeli politician calls for investigation into Netanyahu minister after Gaza flotilla activists allege abuse

children.content: One of Israel's most prominent opposition politicians has called for an investigation into Israel's far-right national security minister, after he posted video showing alleged abuse of activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla last week.

image.altText: Yair Lapid speaks at a lectern gesturing with one hand.

items.title: SA Police return to Yunta as part of Gus Lamont investigation

children.content: Police have returned to Yunta in South Australia's far north as part of their investigation into the disappearance of four-year-old boy Gus Lamont.

image.altText: Gus Lamont

items.title: 170m chimneys come crashing down as power station demolition begins

children.content: The demolition of the Liddell Power Station, which powered homes and businesses in NSW for more than 50 years, is underway.

image.altText: Two chimneys crash together in a controlled demolition creating smoke and dust.
Power Station being demolished.

items.title: Interim AVOs issued to keep developer away from council mayor and GM

children.content: A Southern Highlands property developer is only allowed to communicate with his local council's mayor and general manager through his lawyer under interim AVOs issued against him during a heated court appearance.

image.altText: Man, grey hair, checked jacket, glasses leaving brick building

items.title: 'Incredibly tragic': Mother charged with murder of 4yo girl

children.content: Emergency services were called to a Lowood residence just before 11:10pm on Monday.

image.altText: Police tape in front of a yard with two police officers standing next to a police car.

items.title: Can State of Origin save an NRL season that's losing its way?

children.content: Players, coaches and fans are disillusioned with the rule changes that have come to define this NRL season but ahead of Origin I can the interstate series put a broken season back together?

image.altText: A man is tackled during a State of Origin match

items.title: One world record and a whole lot of regret for Enhanced Games athletes

children.content: Despite the drugs and the super suits, the Enhanced Games delivered little more than a smattering of PBs and a single world fastest time for the hefty price of an athlete's hard-earned credibility.

image.altText: Kristian Gkolomeev,holds up two trophies
